Title: IWS - Verify presence zones configuration UI available
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. Select the "Occupancy Sensor Configuration" tab.
3. Verify Presence Zone 1–5 controls are visible.
Preconditions: 1. A presence zones configuration section is visible.

Title: 2. The UI shows controls for up to 5 presence zones.
3. Each zone has Enabled, Name, and Presence fields matching the control pins: "Presence Zone N Enabled", "Presence Zone N Name", "Presence Zone N Presence".
IWS - Verify default detection zones
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. Select the "Occupancy Sensor Configuration" tab.
3. Review the default zone setup.
Preconditions: 1. A default detection zone is present covering the full sensor field of view.

Title: 2. The zone has a default name and configuration.
IWS - Add a new detection zone
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. Select the "Occupancy Sensor Configuration" tab.
3. Click the <add zone> button or control.
4. Configure the new zone (name, boundaries).
Preconditions: 1. A new detection zone is added to the list.

Title: 2. The zone can be named and its boundaries configured.
3. The zone appears in the zones list.
IWS - Remove a detection zone
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
5. At least two detection zones are configured.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. Select the "Occupancy Sensor Configuration" tab.
3. Select a zone to remove.
4. Click the <remove zone> button or control.
5. Confirm removal if prompted.
Preconditions: 1. The selected zone is removed from the zones list.

Title: 2. The remaining zones are unaffected.
3. Associated control pins for the removed zone are no longer available.
IWS - Rename a detection zone
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
5. At least one detection zone is configured.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. Select the "Occupancy Sensor Configuration" tab.
3. Select a zone and change its name to "Entrance Area".
Preconditions: 1. The zone name updates to "Entrance Area" in the zones list.

Title: 2. Any associated control pins update to reflect the new zone name.
IWS - Verify presence detection per zone
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
5. Multiple detection zones are configured.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. Select the "Occupancy Sensor Configuration" tab.
3. Have a person stand in the area corresponding to Zone 1 only.
4. Observe the presence status for each zone.
2. Other zones show unoccupied/no presence.
3. The per-zone occupancy status updates correctly as the person moves between zones.
IWS - Verify people count per zone
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
5. Multiple detection zones are configured.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. Select the "Occupancy Sensor Configuration" tab.
3. Have a known number of people in the area corresponding to a specific zone.
4. Observe the people count for that zone.
Preconditions: 1. The per-zone people count reflects the approximate number of people in that zone.

Title: 2. The count updates as people enter or leave the zone area.
IWS - Verify zone-specific control pins are exposed
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
5. Multiple detection zones are configured (e.g., Zone 1, Zone 2).
Preconditions: 1. Select the IWS component on Center Workspace > Canvas.

Title: 2. Review the output control pins.
3. Look for zone-specific pins (e.g., "Presence Zone 1 Enabled", "Presence Zone 1 Name", "Presence Zone 1 Presence").
Preconditions: 1. Zone-specific output control pins are exposed for each configured zone.

Title: 2. Each zone has Enabled, Name, and Presence pins.
3. Pin names follow the pattern "Presence Zone N Enabled/Name/Presence".
IWS - Verify maximum of 5 presence zones
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. Select the "Occupancy Sensor Configuration" tab.
3. Enable all 5 presence zones (Presence Zone 1 through Presence Zone 5).
4. Verify no option to add a 6th zone exists.
Preconditions: 1. All 5 presence zones can be enabled and configured.

Title: 2. There is no option to create additional zones beyond 5.
3. The control pins confirm exactly 5 zone sets (Presence Zone 1–5).
IWS - Verify detection zone boundaries do not overlap error handling
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
5. At least one detection zone is configured.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. Select the "Occupancy Sensor Configuration" tab.
3. Add a second zone that overlaps with the first zone boundaries.
Preconditions: 1. Either the overlap is allowed (with expected behavior documented) or the UI displays a warning/validation message about overlapping zones.

Title: 2. If overlapping is allowed, people in the overlap area may be counted in both zones.
IWS - Verify detection zones persist after save and reopen
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. Configure multiple detection zones with custom names and boundaries.
Preconditions: 1. Save the design via Menu Bar > File > Save.

Title: 2. Close the design.
3. Reopen the saved design.
4. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".
5. Select the "Occupancy Sensor Configuration" tab and review zone configuration.
Preconditions: 1. All previously configured detection zones are present.

Title: 2. Zone names, boundaries, and settings are retained exactly as saved.
3. Zone-specific control pins are still exposed correctly.
